creator: jdc@koitsu.org
text:
I just realised a simpler approach might be this:

post-patch:
.if !exists(/usr/lib/libcom_err.so)
        @${REINPLACE_CMD} -e "s,-lcom_err,,g" ${WRKSRC}/configure
.endif

Since this file would exist on FreeBSD 9.x (with or without WITHOUT_KERBEROS=yes in src.conf), but would be excluded on FreeBSD 10.x systems using WITHOUT_KERBEROS=yes.

I'll leave it up to the port maintainer to decide which methodology is preferred, or possibly a combination of multiples.
